title: use bedrock us cross-region profile for nova-fast
author: ElliotEtag
pr_body_excerpt:
- Switches nova-fast (nova-micro) to the US cross-region inference profile `us.amazon.nova-micro-v1:0`, matching `nova-2-lite` and `claude-haiku`.
- Fixes Bedrock throttling: a single peak burst on Jun 3 (07:00–07:30 UTC) produced ~200 `429 Too many tokens` 5xx because nova-micro was pinned to one region. Cross-region inference distributes load across US regions, raising the effective token-per-minute ceiling. No pricing change — Bedrock bills by the calling region.
- Verified end-to-end: real `/v1/chat/completions` call through the gen worker (Portkey → Bedrock) returns 200 with resolved model `us.amazon.nova-micro-v1:0`; 8-way concurrent burst returned 0 5xx; billing path clean (no missing conversion-rate warnings). Resolver unit test updated.
- Also fixes stale references in the model-debugging skill doc: rotated-out Tinybird token, `.tinyb` pointing at staging, and the non-existent `model_errors` pipe (now `recent_server_errors`); adds correct `model_health` columns.

gist.headline: Nova Fast gets a wider lane
gist.blurb: We've switched Nova Fast to use cross-region routing on AWS Bedrock. This means fewer bottlenecks, zero rate-limit hiccups, and smoother chatting all around.
gist.summary: Switched the nova-fast model to use Bedrock's US cross-region inference profile (us.amazon.nova-micro-v1:0) to eliminate 429 throttling errors during peak loads.
gist.impact: Users will experience significantly improved reliability and fewer failed requests when generating text with nova-fast.
gist.visual_concept: The bee mascot and monitor robot in a cozy server room, looking at a glowing network map that spreads across multiple regions, representing cross-region routing and improved traffic flow.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of the cute bee mascot and a chunky monitor-head robot standing in a warm server room. The robot's screen shows a glowing map of connected nodes spreading across regions. Soft lime green (#ecf874) and lavender gradient background. Warm lighting, lo-fi 8-bit aesthetic like Stardew Valley. Nostalgic but beautiful, emotionally warm.
